verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Harness built; Runs unattended; Asserts service + session restore; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the harness absent (5/5 failing), green at HEAD (5/5)
gate_result.harness_evidence: scripts/cold-reboot-harness.mjs implements the two phases around a real reboot: arm captures the expectation manifest (boot_id from /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id, the service set that must come back, the durable session baseline) to durable storage with /run tmpfs refused outright; verify demands a NEW boot_id (same-boot is an early named failure unless the explicit in-session override is given), runs the full ARCHBP-093..098 re-attach, and writes a durable verdict receipt; unit prints the systemd USER unit for the post-boot run.
gate_result.unattended_evidence: verify executes as a stdin-free subprocess in the gate (no TTY, all inputs from files and /proc) and the emitted unit is Type=oneshot, WantedBy=default.target, After= only the user reattach unit, with no Requires= on any host system unit and no TTY/StandardInput directives.
gate_result.restore_evidence: Green fixture path: two health-gated fixture services + a durable fixture session store — verify exits 0 with services.healthy=2 and sessions.restored=true. Failure paths proven loud: a dead service exits 1 with the failed service NAMED in the receipt; a same-boot run without the override exits 1 with verdict same-boot.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Executed against the real production stack: arm captured the live boot_id with the postgresql-ruvector/redb-plane/glass-engine-frontdoor service set and a 10-session durable baseline; verify --allow-same-boot returned ok=true, verdict same-boot-dry-run-pass, services 3/3 healthy, sessions 10/10 restored, exit 0. The production manifest is armed at the durable default path — after the ARCHBP-100 physical reboot, verify (no override) will demand the new boot_id and assert full re-attach.
gate_result.honest_boundary: The harness never reboots the host itself; executing the physical cold-reboot gauntlet is ARCHBP-100 (owner-gated), for which this harness is the instrument.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-099 is complete: the cold-reboot re-attach harness is built and proven — arm/verify phases around a real reboot with boot_id discipline, durable-only state, unattended execution via a decoupled systemd user unit, loud named failures — and a production same-boot dry run passed 3/3 services and 10/10 sessions, leaving the manifest armed for ARCHBP-100's physical reboot.
